text:
There once was a small, cozy town bathed in a perpetual golden hue. The center of this charming locale was a modest pub called The Lantern, where tales and ale mingled in a delightful symphony.

In The Lantern, a young widow named Clara sat at the wooden bar, nursing her third glass of amber liquid. The chatter of the townsfolk ebbed and flowed like the gentle stream outside. A storyteller named Bartholomew leaned against the counter, his weathered face reflecting the glowing fire.

"Tell me another," Clara begged, her voice tender.

Bartholomew shrugged, "How about the time I found a talking mouse?" His eyes twinkled.

Clara giggled, a sound as sweet as the meadows near her home. She ordered another drink as Bartholomew began his tale.

It started back when he was a lad, playing hide-and-seek in a hedge-row. That particular mouse was quite chatty. Bartholomew recounted
